paper_title: Modelling Holocene carbon accumulation and methane emissions of boreal wetlands – an Earth system model approach

methods.description: This method describes the modification of the CBALANCE submodel within JSBACH to create the peatBALANCE model, which is tailored to simulate carbon cycle dynamics in boreal wetlands by integrating wetland-specific processes.

steps.description: Integrate wetland specific parameterizations into the CBALANCE submodel to create peatBALANCE.
steps.searchable_summary: modify CBALANCE model to include wetland-specific parameters

steps.description: Extend the CBALANCE model to include typical wetland processes affecting the carbon cycle.
steps.searchable_summary: extend model to include wetland processes like acrotelm and catotelm layers

steps.description: Integrate the Walter model to consider carbon emissions in the form of CH4.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate Walter model for CH4 emissions

methods.description: This method employs the Walter model to simulate methane emissions through diffusion, ebullition, and plant-mediated transport, coupled with the peatBALANCE model to estimate CH4 and CO2 emissions.

steps.description: Simulate methane emissions using the Walter model, focusing on diffusion, ebullition, and plant-mediated transport.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate CH4 emissions using Walter model pathways

steps.description: Calculate CH4 production within the peatBALANCE model and distribute it to the methane model's root zone.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate and distribute CH4 production in peatBALANCE

steps.description: Couple the methane emission model asynchronously to the peatBALANCE model and perform time slice model experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: couple methane model to peatBALANCE and run experiments

steps.description: Estimate the relation between CO2 and CH4 emissions without feedback to the peatBALANCE model.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate CO2 to CH4 emissions ratio
